ads.description: **Upright Arcade Machine** 2 Player (with lots of simultaneous gameplay) Over 1000 games The Upright Arcade Machine has been a favorite for younger clients or families because two players can play at the same time against each other on multiple games (while the Whiskey Barrel and Cocktail machines have games that go back and forth between player one and two for their turns). A lot of kids aren't used to having to wait their turn to play against someone in video games and this is a great addition to any game room. You can't find machines like this anywhere else. The High Score arcade machines stand out because they are 100% custom-designed and built by a team in Tennessee with years of experience. No prebuilt templates or kits, no stock artwork. The High Score understands that making a machine as easy to operate as possible is essential for rental properties, game rooms, break rooms, and businesses. That's why all High Score machines are all plug-and-play (no complicated start up or shut down) and include easy-access volume control.

ads.author: Ryan Betz
ads.title: Venom Pro Pinball Machine
ads.description: Nearly BRAND NEW Venom. This was the first pin I have ever purchased, I bought this on New Year’s Day 2024. I’ve had multiple since then (that will happen ). It’s got less than 350 plays on it and is in flawless condition and plays perfectly. I just booted it up and installed the latest software update. I also have the topper for it! The topper alone for this machine is $1,200 and it’s incredible. It’s got an LCD screen that has a symbiot in the middle. This topper is also functional, because it unlocks your ability to play as ghost rider in the game. The sound track is also incredible. I really enjoyed playing this game, but it’s time to move onto something else. This is a perfect pin if you’re just getting into pinball. It also has a few subtle mods to it, it’s got board protectors on the sides of both ball locks. It also has a 3d printed daily bugle that replaces the thin plastic one. I’d really like to trade for another machine in the SAME condition. It doesn’t have to be a pro, I can add cash for differences in value. I can keep the topper or include it. This can all be discussed with whoever is interested. I will sell outright. If I sell I’m looking for $5,000 without the topper and $6,000 with. It’s on the first floor and can be moved fairly simply. This is probably the nicest one you will find without buying it new in the box. I’m looking for trades for newer stern machines for the time being. Preferable trades: The walking dead remastered or original AMC edition, or stranger things. Will entertain others as well.

ads.author: Joshua Beecham
ads.title: Arcade Cabinet Project - Cabinet Shell and other parts - $200
ads.description: Empty arcade cabinet shell. Perfect restoration project for an arcade enthusiast, retro gamer, or father-and-son build. The cabinet was built from scratch using 3/4" MDF and includes: 32" portrait-mounted TV with Lexan bezel Marquee fluorescent light Cabinet shell The TV has older inputs including VGA and DVI, and may also have HDMI. Please note: the cabinet has been stored on a screened porch for several years and is dusty. It will need cleaning and some refurbishment before being put back into service. I originally built this for myself and planned to build another one someday, so I removed many of the reusable components when I retired it. It worked perfectly when I disassembled it all. We can some testing from my back porch if you want. Cabinet shell + TV + marquee light: $50 or make offer Optional components available separately: Mini-fridge - $35 Fits perfectly in the bottom of the cabinet. Holds roughly 18-24 cans. Speakers and Amplifier - $25 Generic car speakers and cheap amp. Nothing fancy, but they sounded great in the cabinet. Control Panel - $100 This was the most labor-intensive part of the build. Includes: 12 quality arcade buttons Dual joysticks Trackball Mini-PAC Opti USB controller The Mini-PAC Opti alone sells for around $69 new. The trackball has a cracked plastic piece that will need repair or replacement. Arcade PC - $30 Windows XP machine configured to boot directly into a MAME front end. The hardware itself isn't particularly valuable, but the software setup and configuration took significant time. You could also replace it with a Raspberry Pi running RetroPie or another arcade emulator solution. Pricing Summary Cabinet + TV + marquee light: $50/make offer Mini-fridge: $35 Speakers + amp: $25 Control panel: $100 Arcade PC: $30 Take everything for $200. or make offer The cabinet itself priced low because I need the space. The accessories are the parts I'd originally planned to keep for a future build, but my wife would be thrilled if they disappeared too. Pickup is only available between 6pm and 9pm weekdays and most anytime next weekend. No delivery. You will need your own truck or SUV. I expect this to be gone after Sunday July 26th. This is less of an arcade machine and more of a starting point if you want to *make* one. I offer no guarantees or support.
